contentBlocks.translation: I take refuge in Sri Rama, who is of the nature of pure brahman and who is superior to the best.
contentBlocks.meanings: शुद्ध (śuddha) – pure; ब्रह्म (brahma) – brahman; परात्पर (parātpara) – superior to the best (parāt:than the best | para:superior); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who is of the nature of kala (i.e. master of everyone's destiny) and the supreme lord.
contentBlocks.meanings: कालात्मक (kālātmak) – of the nature of kala (kāla:time, kala | ātmak:nature of); परमेश्वर (parameśvara) – supreme lord (parama:supreme | īśvara:lord); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who sleeps blissfully on the bed of serpent Sesha Naga.
contentBlocks.meanings: शेषतल्पसुखनिद्रित (śeṣatalpasukhanidrita) – who sleeps blissfully (śeṣa:Sesha Naga | talpa:bed | sukha:blissfully | nidrita:sleeps); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose lotus feet is desired by everyone beginning with Brahma to gain immortality.
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्माद्य (brahmādya) – beginning with Brahma (brahma:Brahma | ādya:beginning with); अमर (amara) – immortal; प्रार्थित (prārthita) – desired; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who adorned the dynasty of sun (surya vamsha).
contentBlocks.meanings: चण्डकिरणकुलमण्डन (caṇḍakiraṇakulamaṇḍana) – adornment of the solar dynasty (caṇḍa:fierce | kiraṇa:ray | kula:dynasty | maṇḍana:adornment); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was the illustrious son of king Dasaratha.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीमद् (śrīmad) – illustrious; दशरथनन्दन (daśarathanandana) – son of Dasaratha (daśaratha:Dasaratha | nandana:son); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who brought great joy to Kausalya.
contentBlocks.meanings: कौसल्या (kausalyā) – Kausalya; सुखवर्धन (sukhavardhana) – joy increaser (sukha:joy | vardhana:increaser); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was dear to sage Viswamitra like a great treasure.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वामित्रप्रियधन (viśvāmitrapriyadhana) – dear to sage Viswamitra (viśvāmitra:sage Viswamitra | priya:dear | dhana:treasure); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was the slayer of the terrible demoness Tataka.
contentBlocks.meanings: घोर (ghora) – terrible; ताटकाघातक (tāṭakāghātaka) – slayer of Tataka (tāṭakā:Tataka | ghātaka:slayer); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who brought the downfall of demon Marica and others.
contentBlocks.meanings: मारीच (mārīca) – Marica; आदि (ādi) – and others; निपातक (nipātaka) – downfall bringer; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was the protector of the yajna of sage Viswamitra.
contentBlocks.meanings: कौशिक (kauśika) – of Viśvāmitra; मख (makha) – sacrifice; संरक्षक (saṃrakṣaka) – protector; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who provided deliverance to the venerable Ahalya.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीमद् (śrīmad) – venerable; अहल्योद्धारक (ahalyoddhāraka) – deliverer of Ahalya (ahalya:Ahalya | uddhāraka:deliverer); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was greatly honoured by sage Gautama.
contentBlocks.meanings: गौतममुनिसम्पूजित (gautamamunisampūjita) – honoured by sage Gautama (gautama:Gautama | muni:sage | sampūjita:honoured); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was praised by the devas and the best of sages.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुरमुनिवरगणसंस्तुत (suramunivaragaṇasaṃstuta) – praised by gods and sages (sura:gods | muni:sages | vara:best | gaṇa:group | saṃstuta:praised); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose gentle feet was washed by the boatman (kewat).
contentBlocks.meanings: नाविकधावितमृदुपद (nāvikadhāvitamṛdupada) – whose gentle feet was washed (nāvika:boatman | dhāvita:washed | mṛdu:gentle | pada:feet); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who enchanted the people of Mithila.
contentBlocks.meanings: मिथिला (mithilā) – Mithila; पुर (pura) – city; जन (jana) – people; मोहक (mohaka) – enchanter; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who enhanced the honour of king Janaka.
contentBlocks.meanings: विदेह (videha) – Janaka's kingdom; मानस (mānasa) – mind; रञ्जक (rañjaka) – enhancer; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who broke the bow of the three-eyed Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्र्यम्बक (tryambaka) – three-eyed Shiva (tri:three | ambaka:eye); कार्मुक (kārmuka) – bow; भञ्जक (bhañjaka) – breaker; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: to whom devi Sita offered the garland during bride groom selection.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीता (sītā) – Sita; अर्पित (arpita) – offered; वरमालिका (varamālikā) – bridegroom's garland (vara:bridegroom | mālikā:garland); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who made festive arrangements during marriage.
contentBlocks.meanings: कृतवैवाहिककौतुक (kṛtavaivāhikakautuka) – made festive arrangements (kṛta:made | vaivāhika:marriage-related | kautuka:festive arrangements); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was the destroyer of the pride of Sri Parashurama.
contentBlocks.meanings: भार्गवदर्पविनाशक (bhārgavadarpavināśaka) – destroyer of Parashurama's pride (bhārgava:of Parashurama | darpa:pride | vināśaka:destroyer); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमदयोध्यापालक राम्॥२२॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmadayodhyāpālaka rām ..22..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who was the illustrious king of Ayodhya.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीमद् (śrīmad) – illustrious; अयोध्या (ayodhyā) – Ayodhya; पालक (pālaka) – protector; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was adorned with innumerable virtues.
contentBlocks.meanings: अगणितगुणगणभूषित (agaṇitaguṇagaṇabhūṣita) – adorned with innumerable virtues (agaṇita:innumerable | guṇa:virtues | gaṇa:group | bhūṣita:adorned); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was desired by the daughter of the earth (i.e. devi Sita).
contentBlocks.meanings: अवनीतनया (avanītanayā) – daughter of the earth (avanī:earth | tanayā:daughter); कामित (kāmita) – desired; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose face is like the full moon.
contentBlocks.meanings: राकाचन्द्रसमानन (rākācandrasamānana) – face like the full moon (rākā:full moon | candra:moon | samānana:like face); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who went to the forest following the words of his father.
contentBlocks.meanings: पितृवाक्याश्रितकानन (pitṛvākyāśritakānana) – who went to the forest (pitṛ:father | vākya:words | āśrita:following | kānana:forest); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: to whose feet Guha offered himself as a dear servant.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रिय (priya) – dear; गुह (guha) – Guha; विनिवेदित (vinivedita) – offered; पद (pada) – feet; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose gentle feet was washed (by Guha).
contentBlocks.meanings: तत् (tat) – that; क्षालित (kṣālita) – washed; निज (nija) – own; मृदु (mṛdu) – gentle; पद (pada) – feet; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who made the face of sage Bharatwaja shine with joy.
contentBlocks.meanings: भरद्वाजमुखानन्दक (bharadvājamukhānandaka) – who made Bharadvaja's face joyful (bharadvāja:Bharadvaja | mukha:face | ānandaka:made joyful); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who made the Chitrakuta mountain as his habitat.
contentBlocks.meanings: चित्रकूट (citrakūṭa) – Chitrakuta; अद्रि (adri) – mountain; निकेतन (niketana) – habitat; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who continually thought of his father Dasaratha during his exile in the forest.
contentBlocks.meanings: दशरथसन्ततचिन्तित (daśarathasantatacintita) – thought of by Dasaratha's son (daśaratha:Dasaratha | santata:son | cintita:thought of); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was earnestly requested by Bharata, the son of Kaikeyi to return to the kingdom.
contentBlocks.meanings: कैकेयीतनयार्थित (kaikeyītanayārthita) – requested by Kaikeyi's son (kaikeyī:Kaikeyi | tanaya:son | arthita:requested); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who performed the last rites of his father.
contentBlocks.meanings: विरचित (viracita) – performed; निज (nija) – own; पितृकर्म (pitṛkarma) – father's rites (pitṛ:father | karma:rites); क (ka) – who; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
भरतार्पितनिजपादुक राम्॥३४॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bharatārpitanijapāduka rām ..34..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who gave his paduka (wooden sandal) to Bharata.
contentBlocks.meanings: भरतार्पितनिजपादुक (bharatārpitanijapāduka) – who gave his paduka (bharata:to Bharata | arpita:offered | nija:own | pāduka:wooden sandal); राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who purified the environment of the Dandakaranya forest by slaying the demons.
contentBlocks.meanings: दण्डकवनजनपावन (daṇḍakavanajanapāvana) – purifier of Dandaka forest (daṇḍaka:Dandaka | vana:forest | jana:people | pāvana:purifier); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who destroyed the wicked demon Viradha.
contentBlocks.meanings: दुष्टविराधविनाशन (duṣṭavirādhavināśana) – destroyer of wicked Viradha (duṣṭa:wicked | virādha:Viradha | vināśana:destroyer); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was worshipped by sage Sarabhanga and sage Sutheekshana.
contentBlocks.meanings: शरभङ्गसुतीक्ष्णार्चित (śarabhaṅgasutīkṣṇārcita) – worshipped by Sarabhanga and Sutheekshana (śarabhaṅga:sage Sarabhanga | sutīkṣṇa:sage Sutheekshana | arcita:worshipped); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was full with the blessings of sage Agastya.
contentBlocks.meanings: अगस्त्य (agastya) – sage Agastya; अनुग्रहवर्धित (anugrahavardhita) – full with blessings (anugraha:blessings | vardhita:increased); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was served by the king of vultures (Jatayu).
contentBlocks.meanings: गृध्राधिप (gṛdhrādhipa) – king of vultures (gṛdhra:vulture | ādhipa:king); संसेवित (saṃsevita) – served by; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who abided at the bank of the river at Panchavati.
contentBlocks.meanings: पञ्चवटीतटसुस्थित (pañcavaṭītaṭasusthita) – abided at Panchavati bank (pañcavaṭī:Panchavati | taṭa:bank | su:well | sthita:abided); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who enjoyed the pain on Surpanakha for her wicked intentions.
contentBlocks.meanings: शूर्पणखा (śūrpaṇakhā) – Surpanakha; अर्ति (arti) – pain; विधायक (vidhāyaka) – causing; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who destroyed the face of demons Khara and Dushana from the world.
contentBlocks.meanings: खर (khara) – Khara; दूषण (dūṣaṇa) – Dushana; मुख (mukha) – face; सूदक (sūdaka) – destroyer; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who went after the deer wanted by devi Sita.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीता (sītā) – Sita; प्रिय (priya) – beloved; हरिण (hariṇa) – deer; अनुग (anuga) – follower; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who inflicted pain on Marica with his arrow for his (Marica's) wrong deed.
contentBlocks.meanings: मारीच (mārīca) – Marica; अर्ति (arti) – pain; कृत् (kṛt) – inflicted; आशुग (āśuga) – with arrow; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who searched for the lost Sita earnestly.
contentBlocks.meanings: विनष्टसीतान्वेषक (vinaṣṭasītānveṣaka) – searcher of lost Sita (vinaṣṭa:lost | sītā:Sita | anveṣaka:searcher); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who gave liberation to the king of vultures (Jatayu).
contentBlocks.meanings: गृध्राधिप (gṛdhrādhipa) – king of vultures (gṛdhra:vulture | adhipa:king); गतिदायक (gatidāyaka) – giver of liberation (gati:liberation | dāyaka:giver); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who ate the fruit given by Shabari as an offering of devotion.
contentBlocks.meanings: शबरीदत्त (śabarīdattaphalāśana) – given by Shabari (śabarī:Shabari | datta:given); फलाशन (phalāśana) – fruit eating (phala:fruit | āśana:eating); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
कबन्धबाहुच्छेदक राम्॥४८॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kabandhabāhucchedaka rām ..48..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who cut off the arms of Kabandha.
contentBlocks.meanings: कबन्ध (kabandha) – Kabandha; बाहुच्छेदक (bāhucchedaka) – arm cutter (bāhu:arm | cchedaka:cutter); राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose feet was served by Hanuman.
contentBlocks.meanings: हनुमत् (hanumat) – Hanuman; सेवित (sevita) – served; निज (nija) – own; पद (pada) – feet; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who granted the wish of Sugriva Who bowed to him in surrender.
contentBlocks.meanings: नत (nata) – bowed; सुग्रीव (sugrīva) – Sugriva; अभीष्ट (abhīṣṭa) – wish; द (da) – granted; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who destroyed the proud bali, the king of monkeys.
contentBlocks.meanings: गर्वित (garvita) – proud; वालि (vāli) – Bali; संहारक (saṃhāraka) – destroyer; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who sent a monkey as messenger (to Ravana).
contentBlocks.meanings: वानरदूतप्रेषक (vānaradūtapreṣaka) – who sent a monkey messenger (vānara:monkey | dūta:messenger | preṣaka:sender); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
हितकरलक्ष्मणसंयुत राम्॥५३॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hitakaralakṣmaṇasaṃyuta rām ..53..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who was always united with lakshmana Who served him earnestly.
contentBlocks.meanings: हितकरलक्ष्मणसंयुत (hitakaralakṣmaṇasaṃyuta) – united with beneficial Lakshmana (hitakara:beneficial | lakṣmaṇa:Lakshmana | saṃyuta:united with); राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was continually remembered by Hanuman, the most excellent among the monkeys.
contentBlocks.meanings: कपिवर (kapivara) – best among monkeys (kapi:monkey | vara:best); सन्तत (santata) – continually; संस्मृत (saṃsmṛta) – remembered; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who removed the obstacles to the movement of Hanuman.
contentBlocks.meanings: तत् (tat) – that; गति (gati) – movement; विघ्न (vighna) – obstacles; ध्वंसक (dhvaṃsaka) – remover; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was the support of the life of devi Sita.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीताप्राणाधारक (sītāprāṇādhāraka) – support of Sita's life (sītā:Sita | prāṇa:life | ādhāraka:support); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who despised the wicked ten-headed Ravana.
contentBlocks.meanings: दुष्ट (duṣṭa) – wicked; दशानन (daśānana) – ten-headed (daśa:ten | ānana:head); दूषित (dūṣita) – despised; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who adorned the wise and eminent Hanuman with praises.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिष्ट (śiṣṭa) – wise; हनूमत् (hanūmat) – Hanuman; भूषित (bhūṣita) – adorned; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who heard the kakasura incident which took place in theforest from Hanuman as told (to Hanuman) by devi Sita.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीता (sītā) – Sita; वेदित (vedita) – told; काक (kāka) – crow; आवन (āvana) – forest; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who saw the chudamani of devi Sita which was brought by Hanuman.
contentBlocks.meanings: कृत (kṛta) – made; चूडामणि (cūḍāmaṇi) – crest jewel (cūḍā:crest | maṇi:jewel); दर्शन (darśana) – seeing; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
कपिवरवचनाश्वासित राम्॥६१॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kapivaravacanāśvāsita rām ..61..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who was comforted by the words of Hanuman, the most excellent of monkeys.
contentBlocks.meanings: कपिवरवचनाश्वासित (kapivaravacanāśvāsita) – comforted by Hanuman's words (kapi:monkey | vara:excellent | vacana:words | āśvāsita:comforted); राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who set out for destroying Ravana.
contentBlocks.meanings: रावणनिधनप्रस्थित (rāvaṇanidhanaprasthita) – set out to destroy Ravana (rāvaṇa:Ravana | nidhana:destruction | prasthita:set out); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was surrounded by the army of monkeys.
contentBlocks.meanings: वानर (vānara) – monkey; सैन्य (sainya) – army; समावृत (samāvṛta) – surrounded; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who requested the king of ocean to give way and subsequently threatened to dry him.
contentBlocks.meanings: शोषित (śoṣita) – dried up; सरिदीश (saridīśa) – king of rivers (sarit:river | īśa:king); अर्थित (arthita) – requested; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who gave assurance of fearlessness to Vibhisana when he took refuge.
contentBlocks.meanings: विभीषण (vibhīṣaṇa) – Vibhisana; अभयदायक (abhayadāyaka) – giver of fearlessness (abhaya:fearlessness | dāyaka:giver); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who created a bridge across the sea by binding rocks.
contentBlocks.meanings: पर्वतसेतुनिबन्धक (parvatasetunibandhaka) – who bound a bridge of mountains (parvata:mountain | setu:bridge | nibandhaka:binder); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who cut off the head of Kumbhakarna in the battle.
contentBlocks.meanings: कुम्भकर्ण (kumbhakarṇa) – Kumbhakarna; शिरच्छेदक (śiracchedaka) – head cutter (śiras:head | chedaka:cutter); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who crushed the army of demons in the battle.
contentBlocks.meanings: राक्षससङ्घविमर्दक (rākṣasasaṅghavimardaka) – crusher of demon army (rākṣasa:demon | saṅgha:group, army | vimardaka:crusher); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who destroyed Mahiravana (through Hanuman) who trapped him in patala loka.
contentBlocks.meanings: अहि (ahi) – snake; महि (mahi) – earth; रावण (rāvaṇa) – Ravana; चारण (cāraṇa) – movement; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who destroyed the ten-faced Ravana in the battle.
contentBlocks.meanings: संहृत (saṃhṛta) – destroyed; दशमुख (daśamukha) – ten-faced (daśa:ten | mukha:face); रावण (rāvaṇa) – Ravana; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was praised by Brahma, Shiva and other devas.
contentBlocks.meanings: विधि (vidhi) – Brahma; भव (bhava) – Shiva; मुख (mukha) – face; सुर (sura) – devas; संस्तुत (saṃstuta) – praised; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose divine deeds were seen by Dasaratha from heaven.
contentBlocks.meanings: खस्थित (khasthita) – situated in the sky (kha:sky | sthita:situated); दशरथ (daśaratha) – Dasaratha; वीक्षित (vīkṣita) – seen; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was delighted seeing devi Sita after the battle.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीता (sītā) – Sita; दर्शन (darśana) – seeing; मोदित (modita) – delighted; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was reverentially saluted by Vibhisana who (Vibhisana) was coronated by him.
contentBlocks.meanings: अभिषिक्तविभीषणनत (abhiṣiktavibhīṣaṇanata) – saluted by Vibhisana (abhiṣikta:coronated | vibhīṣaṇa:Vibhisana | nata:saluted); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who ascended the pushpaka vimana for returning to Ayodhya.
contentBlocks.meanings: पुष्पकयानारोहण (puṣpakayānārohaṇa) – ascended the Pushpaka vimana (puṣpaka:Pushpaka | yāna:vehicle | ārohaṇa:ascending); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who visited sage Bharadwaja and other sages.
contentBlocks.meanings: भरद्वाज (bharadvāja) – Bharadwaja; आदि (ādi) – and others; निषेवण (niṣevaṇa) – visiting; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who brought joy to the life of Bharata after his return.
contentBlocks.meanings: भरतप्राणप्रियकर (bharataprāṇapriyakara) – who pleases Bharata's life (bharata:Bharata | prāṇa:life | priya:dear | kara:maker); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who adorned the city of Ayodhya after his return from Lanka.
contentBlocks.meanings: साकेतपुरीभूषण (sāketapurībhūṣaṇa) – ornament of Saket city another name for Ayodhya (sāketa:Saket | purī:city | bhūṣaṇa:ornament); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was offered colour marks by Vibhisana during the coronation ceremony.
contentBlocks.meanings: सकल (sakala) – all; स्वीय (svīya) – own; समानत (samānata) – equally; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who showered his grace on the sun dynasty (surya vamsha).
contentBlocks.meanings: रत्नलसत्पीठास्थित (ratnalasatpīṭhāsthita) – seated on a jeweled throne (ratna:jewel | lasat:shining | pīṭha:throne | āsthita:seated); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who treated everyone equally as his own people.
contentBlocks.meanings: पट्टाभिषेकालङ्कृत (paṭṭābhiṣekālaṅkṛta) – adorned with coronation (paṭṭa:coronation | abhiṣeka:anointment | alaṅkṛta:adorned); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was seated on the throne studded with shining jewels.
contentBlocks.meanings: पार्थिवकुलसम्मानित (pārthivakulasammānita) – honored by royal families (pārthiva:royal | kula:family | sammānita:honored); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was adorned with the royal crown during coronation.
contentBlocks.meanings: विभीषण (vibhīṣaṇa) – Vibhishana; अर्पित (arpita) – offered; रङ्गक (raṅgaka) – crown; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who honoured the assembly of kings during his coronation.
contentBlocks.meanings: कीशकुलानुग्रहकर (kīśakulānugrahakara) – benefactor of the Kīśa clan (kīśa:Kīśa clan | kula:clan | anugraha:favor, benefaction | kara:doer, maker); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who is the guardian of all living beings.
contentBlocks.meanings: सकलजीवसंरक्षक (sakalajīvasaṃrakṣaka) – guardian of all living beings (sakala:all | jīva:living beings | saṃrakṣaka:guardian); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तलोकाधारक राम्॥८६॥
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastalokādhāraka rām ..86..
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the supporter of all the worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: समस्तलोकाधारक (samastalokādhāraka) – supporter of all worlds (samasta:all | loka:worlds | ādhāraka:supporter); राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was praised by all the visiting sages.
contentBlocks.meanings: आगत (āgata) – arrived; मुनिगण (munigaṇa) – group of sages (muni:sage | gaṇa:group); संस्तुत (saṃstuta) – praised; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Whose fame spread far and wide in the ten directions in the voices of all.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्रुत (viśruta) – famous; दश (daśa) – ten; कण्ठ (kaṇṭha) – throats; उद्भव (udbhava) – origin; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was happy in the embrace of devi Sita.
contentBlocks.meanings: सीता (sītā) – Sita; अलिङ्गन (āliṅgana) – embrace; निर्वृत (nirvṛta) – happy; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who protected his empire by moral precepts (i.e. dharma).
contentBlocks.meanings: नीतिसुरक्षितजनपद (nītisurakṣitajanapada) – empire protected by moral precepts (nīti:moral precepts | surakṣita:protected | janapada:empire); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who abandoned devi Sita ( born of Janaka ) in the forest.
contentBlocks.meanings: विपिन (vipina) – forest; त्याजित (tyājita) – abandoned; जनकज (janakaja) – born of Janaka (janaka:Janaka | ja:born of); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who caused the killing of demon Lavanasura (through brother Satrughna).
contentBlocks.meanings: कारित (kārita) – caused; लवणासुरवध (lavaṇāsuravadha) – killing of demon Lavanasura (lavaṇa:Lavanasura | asura:demon | vadha:killing); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was praised by Shambuka who had gone to heaven.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वर्गत (svargata) – gone to heaven (svarga:heaven | gata:gone); शम्बुक (śambuka) – Shambuka; संस्तुत (saṃstuta) – praised; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who made happy his own sons Lava and Kusha.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्व (sva) – own; तनय (tanaya) – sons; कुशल (kuśala) – happy; लव (lava) – Lava; नन्दित (nandita) – made happy; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was initiated into the sacrifice of Ashwamedha.
contentBlocks.meanings: अश्वमेधक्रतुदीक्षित (aśvamedhakratudīkṣita) – initiated into Ashwamedha sacrifice (aśvamedha:Ashwamedha sacrifice | kratu:sacrifice | dīkṣita:initiated); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was made to know his divine position by kala when his time to depart arrived.
contentBlocks.meanings: कालावेदित (kālāvedita) – made known by time (kāla:time | āvedita:made known); सुरपद (surapada) – divine position (sura:divine | pada:position); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who gave liberation to the people of Ayodhya.
contentBlocks.meanings: आयोध्यक (āyodhyaka) – of Ayodhya (āyodhyā:Ayodhya | ka:of); जन (jana) – people; मुक्तिद (muktida) – gave liberation (mukti:liberation | da:giver); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who made the faces of Brahma and other gods shine with joy.
contentBlocks.meanings: विधिमुखविबुधानन्दकर (vidhimukhavibudhānandakara) – who made faces shine with joy (vidhi:Brahma | mukha:face | vibudha:gods | ānanda:joy | kara:maker); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who assumed his own divine form shining with light during his departure.
contentBlocks.meanings: तेजोमय (tejomaya) – full of light (tejas:light | maya:full of); निजरूपक (nijarūpaka) – own divine form (nija:own | rūpaka:form); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who releases one from the bondage of samsara (worldly attachments).
contentBlocks.meanings: संसृति (saṃsṛti) – worldly existence; बन्ध (bandha) – bondage; विमोचक (vimocaka) – releaser; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who is eager to establish dharma in the world.
contentBlocks.meanings: धर्मस्थापनतत्पर (dharmasthāpanatatpara) – eager to establish dharma (dharma:righteousness | sthāpana:establishment | tatpara:eager); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who gives liberation to one wholly devoted to him.
contentBlocks.meanings: भक्तिपरायणम् (bhaktiparāyaṇam) – wholly devoted (bhakti:devotion | parāyaṇam:wholly devoted); मुक्तिद (muktida) – gives liberation (mukti:liberation | da:giver); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who is the guardian of all moving and non-moving beings.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वचराचरपालक (sarvacarācarapālaka) – guardian of all moving and non-moving beings (sarva:all | cara:moving | acara:non-moving | pālaka:guardian); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who restrains all diseases of worldly attachments from his devotees.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वभवामयवारक (sarvabhavāmayavāraka) – restrainer of all worldly diseases (sarva:all | bhava:worldly existence | āmaya:diseases | vāraka:restrainer); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who established himself in the abode of Vaikunatha after his departure.
contentBlocks.meanings: वैकुण्ठालयसंस्थित (vaikuṇṭhālaya-saṃsthita) – established in Vaikuntha's abode (vaikuṇṭha:Vaikuntha | ālaya:abode | saṃsthita:established); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: Who was established in his divine position of eternal blissafter his departure.
contentBlocks.meanings: नित्यानन्दपदस्थित (nityānandapadasthita) – established in eternal bliss (nitya:eternal | ānanda:bliss | pada:position | sthita:established); राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: O Rama, Sri Rama, victory to you king Rama.
contentBlocks.meanings: राम् (rām) – O Rama; राम् (rām) – Sri Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.translation: O Rama, Sri Rama, victory to you Sita Rama.
contentBlocks.meanings: राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama;

contentBlocks.original:
राम् राम् जय राजा राम्।
राम् राम् जय सीता राम्॥
॥इति नामरामायणम् सम्पूर्णम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rām rām jaya rājā rām.
rām rām jaya sītā rām..
..iti nāmarāmāyaṇam sampūrṇam..
contentBlocks.translation:
O Rama, Sri Rama, victory to you king Rama.
O Rama, Sri Rama, victory to you Sita Rama.
contentBlocks.meanings: राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; राजा (rājā) – king; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; राम् (rām) – Rama; जय (jaya) – victory; सीता (sītā) – Sita; राम् (rām) – Rama; इति (iti) – thus; नामरामायणम् (nāmarāmāyaṇam) – Name of Ramayana (nāma:name | rāmāyaṇam:Ramayana); सम्पूर्णम् (sampūrṇam) – complete;
